file_insights.purpose: One-shot systemd service to run bop merge-gate operation

patterns_discovered: Standard systemd service unit structure ([Unit], [Service] sections)
One-shot service pattern for non-repeating background operations
Absolute path invocation of compiled binary with long-option arguments
Minimal configuration approach (relies on systemd defaults)
gotchas_discovered: Hardcoded binary path /usr/local/bin/bop assumes standard installation location - installation script must match
No User/Group directives - service runs as root (potential security concern)
No error handling or restart policy - transient failures will silently exit
No WorkingDirectory specified - inherits systemd caller's cwd
No logging configuration (StandardOutput/StandardError) - output goes to journal only
No environment variable passing mechanism visible - card state access depends on cwd or explicit paths
recommendations: Add User=bop-service and Group=bop-service for privilege separation
Document /usr/local/bin/bop installation requirement in setup instructions
Consider adding StandardOutput=journal StandardError=journal for explicit logging
Add RemainAfterExit=yes if service state should persist after exit
Add EnvironmentFile= or Environment= directives if merge-gate needs config paths
Consider adding Before/After ordering directives if this integrates with other system services
For production, consider adding Type=notify and sd_notify() calls for better systemd integration
